summary: Added `tools/functions.sh`, a sourceable shell utility file for recurring decomp workflow tasks: global symbol replacement, reversing replacements, renaming `func_XXXXXXXX`/`lbl_XXXXXXXX` module symbols with a prefix, generating include guards and basic headers, renaming headers while updating guards and include paths, converting small hex literals to decimal, qalc aliases, and disabling Wine debug output. The PR body explicitly frames it as a shared home for frequently used helper functions.
what_changed: Created a new executable shell script at `tools/functions.sh` intended to be sourced by developers.

Added `replace_symbol`, which escapes slashes and dots in the target name and runs `sed -i` over files under `asm`, `src`, and `include`, plus Makefiles and `*.c`, `*.h`, `*.s`, `*.mk` files, using word-boundary replacement.
Added `reverse_symbol`, a simple wrapper that swaps the old and new symbol arguments for undoing or reversing a previous rename.

Added `rename_module_symbols_asm`, which scans an assembly file for labels exactly matching `func_XXXXXXXX:` or `lbl_XXXXXXXX:` and replaces them with a caller-provided prefix plus the address suffix.

Added `rename_module_symbols_c`, which scans C declarations for `func_XXXXXXXX` or `lbl_XXXXXXXX`, including optional `static`, `inline`, and `asm` prefixes, and replaces them with a caller-provided prefix plus the address suffix.
Added include-guard helpers: `get_guard` converts a path into an uppercase underscore guard while stripping a leading `src_`, and `get_include_guard` emits matching `#ifndef`/`#define` lines.
Added `rename_header`, which moves a header file, rewrites its include guard, and globally replaces the old relative include path with the new one.
Added `gen_header`, which creates or prepends a minimal header containing an include guard, `#include <dolphin/types.h>`, and `#endif`.
Added `convert_hex`, which uses Perl bigint mode to replace small hexadecimal literals with decimal values in-place.
Added `qd` and `qx` aliases for decimal and hexadecimal `qalc` output, and exported `WINEDEBUG=-all`.
smart_moves: Centralized ad hoc decomp helper commands into a shared `tools/functions.sh` file instead of leaving them as private shell snippets.
Made symbol renaming reusable across the common decomp locations `asm`, `src`, and `include`, reducing manual search-and-replace risk.
Used regexes that specifically target unresolved decomp-style names such as `func_XXXXXXXX` and `lbl_XXXXXXXX`, which keeps module-prefix renames focused on placeholder symbols.
Escaped `/` and `.` before constructing the `sed` replacement expression in `replace_symbol`, which matters for include paths and dotted identifiers.
Included header-renaming support that updates both the physical file path and the include guard, addressing two common follow-up edits in one workflow.
Provided a reverse operation for symbol replacement, making trial renames easier to back out.
decomp_lessons: Common decomp cleanup tasks, especially placeholder-symbol renames and header moves, benefit from being scripted because they touch many files and are easy to perform inconsistently by hand.
When replacing symbols globally, constrain the search to known source/build file types and use boundary-aware matching to avoid accidental partial-name replacements.
Module-level placeholder renames can be automated by deriving the stable address suffix from `func_XXXXXXXX` or `lbl_XXXXXXXX` and applying a semantic module prefix.
Header generation should standardize include guards and basic includes so new headers start from a consistent project shape.
Header renames should update include paths and guards together; otherwise future matching or style cleanup may be polluted by stale names.
Small utility scripts can encode local project naming conventions and reduce friction for contributors performing repetitive matching work.
naming_conventions: Unresolved function and label symbols use `func_XXXXXXXX` and `lbl_XXXXXXXX`, where `XXXXXXXX` is an uppercase hexadecimal address.
Module-renamed unresolved symbols are formed as `<module_prefix>_<address_suffix>` by replacing the `func_` or `lbl_` prefix with a caller-supplied prefix.
Include guards are derived from file paths by replacing `/` and `.` with `_`, removing a leading `src_`, and uppercasing the result.
The helper assumes source-relative include paths when renaming headers via `realpath --relative-to="src"`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For assembly files, `rename_module_symbols_asm` extracts only labels matching `^(func|lbl)_([0-9A-F]{8})\b:$`, then passes each old and new name through `replace_symbol`.
For C files, `rename_module_symbols_c` extracts placeholder declarations with optional `static`, `inline`, and `asm` prefixes before replacing all occurrences.
`replace_symbol` uses `sed` word boundaries around the escaped target text to reduce accidental replacement of substrings inside longer names.
`convert_hex` uses `perl -Mbigint` to rewrite hexadecimal literals matching `0x[\da-f]{1,7}U?` into decimal, likely for source normalization or matching cleanup.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human rationale provided was the PR body: the file can be sourced to access frequently used utility functions, and other contributors were invited to add useful functions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `tools/functions.sh` to see which helper functions became standard workflow and which were corrected.
Search for uses or references to `rename_module_symbols_asm` and `rename_module_symbols_c` in contributor notes or subsequent tooling changes.
Check later review history for portability fixes, since this script uses `#!/bin/sh` while containing shell constructs such as `function` and `[[ ... ]]` that may depend on the user's shell.
Look for later PRs involving header renames to see whether `rename_header` or the include-guard convention influenced project practice.
